V-M-064.purpose: Verify Phase-46 registration password example and MTProto user-cabinet link/status UX polish.
V-M-064.checks: Password example matches policy, MTProto Telegram app and browser links are separated, visible labels are Russian, status badge refresh is dynamic, and protected deploy surfaces remain unchanged.

commands.command: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-46.xml docs/modules/M-064.xml docs/verification/V-M-064.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml

module-checks.check-1: Registration form renders a password example and keeps passwordStrengthIssues as the validation gate.
module-checks.check-2: Dashboard builds tg://proxy for Telegram app opening and https://t.me/proxy for Ссылка browser/copy action.
module-checks.check-3: Visible MTProto field/action labels are Ссылка, Сервер, Порт, and Секрет.
module-checks.check-4: MTProto status badge class is derived from live payload state and the query has bounded refresh or equivalent invalidation.
module-checks.check-5: Phase-46 smoke fails if deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose surfaces are changed.

verification-levels.module-local: Deterministic static smoke validates exact UX contract: safe password example, passwordStrengthIssues gate, dual MTProto link schemes, Russian labels, bounded status refresh, and no secret-bearing output.
verification-levels.wave-level: Frontend build validates touched React/TypeScript surfaces and shared password policy imports.
verification-levels.phase-level: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and deploy/install diff guard validate artifact consistency and operational isolation.

scenarios.scenario-1: A registering user sees a clear password example and still cannot submit a weak password.
scenarios.scenario-2: An owner with an activated MTProto proxy can open Telegram through tg:// and copy browser link through https://t.me.
scenarios.scenario-3: Pending or degraded MTProto state becomes visible after bounded refetch without a page reload or stale success badge.
scenarios.scenario-4: The UI reuses https://t.me for the Telegram app button or tg:// for the browser/full-link action.
scenarios.scenario-5: Status badge remains statically green after backend state changes or no bounded refresh exists.
scenarios.scenario-6: Smoke, console traces, or fixtures expose a real MTProto secret-bearing link, access token, reset token, or private environment value.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Runtime telemetry is not required for Phase-46 because the scope is frontend UX; static smoke must still assert that no secret-bearing console/debug output is introduced.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Status refresh evidence must be visible in code through bounded polling, invalidation, or equivalent refetch control; unbounded intervals are rejected.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Smoke output must not print real MTProto secrets, full proxy links from live accounts, access tokens, refresh tokens, reset tokens, or private env values.

V-M-064.autonomous-execution-safety: Allowed after plan approval because scope is frontend-only and deploy/install/backend runtime surfaces are out of scope.
